overview: Starter Town is the project's main service hub, packing trade, crafting, fishing, smithing, and runecrafting into one compact region.
Its route layout is intentionally dense, with the castle, general store, smithing hall, ponds, groves, mines, and altars all close enough to support quick loop testing.
The world exports the broadest early-game resource coverage in the bundle, so it reads as the default place to learn how systems feed into one another.
whyItMatters: This is the clearest onboarding world because it shows the full core loop: gather, refine, craft, sell, and travel without long detours.
It anchors the economy layer with merchants for tools, supplies, ores, gems, runecrafting stock, and buyback services.
It also exposes the widest set of skill routes, which makes it the best reference point for understanding how the rest of the world content hangs together.
howToGetStarted: Begin at the default spawn, then check the nearby merchants before branching to the mine, pond, grove, or altar line.
Use the smithing hall to turn early ore into bars, then convert those bars into armor, weapons, or Crafting bases.
Treat the woodcutting routes and altar routes as parallel progression tracks: one feeds handles, shafts, and staffs, while the other feeds rune production.
connectedSystems: Services: general store, fishing supplier, fishing teacher, crafting teacher, Tanner Rusk, Borin Ironvein, Thrain Deepforge, Elira Gemhand, Rune Tutor, and Combination Sage.
Routes: starter mine, iron mine, coal mine, precious mine, gem mine, rune essence mine, starter grove, oak path, willow bend, maple ridge, yew frontier, and the four altar routes.
Skills: cooking, fishing, mining, runecrafting, and woodcutting.
nextSteps: Use Starter Town as the baseline world for comparing resource density, merchant coverage, and route spacing.
Cross-check its ore, fish, wood, and rune-adjacent loops against the item pages they unlock.
After the hub is clear, follow the road connection to North Road Camp and compare the more compact outpost layout.

overview: North Road Camp is a smaller forward region that compresses the same core skill families into a tighter outpost layout.
Its quarry, brazier, pine loop, and caravan point sit close together, so it reads as a faster field-test version of the starter loop.
The gated crystal seam gives the camp a clear progression wall, which makes the region feel like a checkpoint rather than a duplicate of Starter Town.
whyItMatters: This world proves the project can support a second playable region without rebuilding the entire hub structure.
It combines minimal service coverage with enough resources to stay useful, which is important for testing sparse-but-functional world design.
The locked gem seam adds a concrete progression hook and makes the camp valuable both early and later.
howToGetStarted: Arrive through the caravan guide, then use the outpost furnace and anvil to turn nearby ore into usable smithing stock.
Clear the quarry first for copper, tin, and clay, then return later for the crystal seam once the gate is unlocked.
The pine loop and camp pond keep the region productive even before the gated mine opens, so you can gather while you train.
connectedSystems: Services: shopkeeper, caravan guide, Prospector Dain, furnace, and anvil.
Routes: outpost quarry, crystal seam, pine loop, camp pond bank, outpost brazier, and breeze shrine.
Skills: cooking, fishing, mining, runecrafting, and woodcutting.
nextSteps: Use North Road Camp as the field-test region for early mining and travel.
Compare its route density to Starter Town so the difference between a hub and an outpost stays obvious.
Return after progression to unlock the crystal seam and verify the gated resource path.
